proof_sketch: The mutation changes `shared_sq_sum` size from 256 to 255. The kernel uses 256 threads per block; thread tid=255 writes to index 255, which is out-of-bounds. However, the same memory location (offset 1024+255*4) is also read during the reduction (when stride=128, tid=127). In the original, this location is valid (index 255 of a 256-element array). In the mutant, it is beyond the allocated 511 floats, but the write and read still target the same physical address. No other data is corrupted because the location is not used by any declared variable. Under the fixed-shape contract and the tested hardware, the numeric computation remains bit-identical for all inputs.
